name: AI Client (Bridge)
description: Local agent bridge — connects Claude/Codex CLI sessions to the backend via WebSocket

settings.label: Hook Server Port
settings.description: Port for Claude Code PreToolUse hooks (0 = auto-assign)

settings.label: Shared bridge
settings.description: Run unscoped so the bridge serves any user. Leave off to bind to the launcher's logged-in user.

settings.label: Task Timeout (s)
settings.description: Maximum seconds per task before timeout

settings.label: Per-session MCP credentials (lift __bridge__ sentinel)
settings.description: The agent pool already spawns one Claude/Codex subprocess per chat session, but in HTTP MCP transport they all present the same static bearer token — so the MCP server can't tell them apart and log_work / other identity-resolving tools fall back to the '__bridge__' sentinel. When on, the bridge mints a per-(chat session, agent type) JWT with chat_session_id claims at dispatch time and writes a per-subprocess MCP HTTP config with that JWT in the Authorization header. Subprocess count is unchanged. Default off during rollout — flip on per environment as the new backend endpoint deploys.

settings.label: Built-in tools — require approval
settings.description: Ticked built-in tools prompt for approval before running; unticked tools run silently. Empty = all built-ins run without prompting.

settings.label: MCP tools — require approval
settings.description: Same polarity as built-ins: ticked MCP tools prompt for approval (Claude AND Codex); unticked tools run silently. Enforced in the MCP server, applied live (no restart). Operation-aware: a ticked group prompts only on its WRITE operations (create/update/delete) — reads run silently. Pin a single operation with 'group::endpoint_id'. Empty = nothing prompts.

settings.label: Additional accessible directories
settings.description: Extra filesystem roots the agent may read/write beyond the project directory (the agent is otherwise confined to the repo). Add absolute paths, one per entry. The project dir and ~/.claude/plans are always included. Applied to .claude/settings.json on the next apply.
